Vulnerability: No health check on tshark subprocess; silent failure if interface goes down
Control / Mitigation: Implement process watchdog with auto-restart; add heartbeat logging every 60s; alert on zero-packet windows
Detection Method: Monitor tshark PID and packet-count delta

Vulnerability: Parser does not sanitize tshark output; crafted packets could produce CSV fields that break parsing
Control / Mitigation: Validate CSV schema before processing; reject rows with unexpected column counts; log and quarantine malformed files
Detection Method: Schema validation errors in parse log

Vulnerability: Static thresholds may miss low-and-slow attacks or attacks just below threshold
Control / Mitigation: Tune thresholds quarterly based on baseline traffic analysis; add ML anomaly detection in Phase 5; implement rule coverage reporting
Detection Method: Periodic detection gap analysis; red team exercises

Vulnerability: Thresholds too sensitive for network conditions; operators ignore real alerts
Control / Mitigation: Implement adaptive baselines per-source-IP; add analyst feedback loop (Phase 6) to tune scores; provide alert suppression for known-good IPs
Detection Method: Track false positive rate per rule in dashboard stats

Vulnerability: No orchestration or retry logic; single component failure silently drops alerts
Control / Mitigation: Add pipeline health endpoint; implement dead-letter queue for failed AI calls; retry with exponential backoff; add pipeline_status table to SQLite
Detection Method: Pipeline health dashboard widget; dead-letter queue depth metric

Vulnerability: LLM may generate plausible but wrong classifications or risk scores
Control / Mitigation: Enforce structured JSON output with schema validation; cross-check MITRE IDs against known technique list; implement confidence thresholds; log all raw AI responses for audit
Detection Method: Schema validation failures; MITRE ID validation; analyst overrides tracked

Vulnerability: Crafted packet payloads or log content could manipulate AI behavior when included in the analysis prompt
Control / Mitigation: Sanitize all log/packet data before embedding in prompts; truncate payload fields to 256 chars; use fixed system prompt; validate output structure
Detection Method: Output format deviation detection; prompt audit logging

Vulnerability: External API dependency with no SLA in lab environment
Control / Mitigation: Implement API call retry with backoff; queue alerts during outage; add local fallback rule-based scorer for critical alerts
Detection Method: API response time monitoring; error rate tracking

Vulnerability: No input validation on DB writes; concurrent writes may corrupt WAL; no backup mechanism
Control / Mitigation: Enable WAL mode; validate all dict fields before save_alert(); implement daily automated backup; add integrity check on startup
Detection Method: Startup integrity check; backup job status monitoring

Vulnerability: Dashboard runs on 0.0.0.0:5000 with no authentication
Control / Mitigation: Add basic auth or token-based access control; bind to 127.0.0.1 unless explicitly exposed; add session management with timeout; implement CSRF tokens
Detection Method: Access log review; unauthorized request monitoring

Vulnerability: AI-generated report content rendered in templates without proper escaping
Control / Mitigation: Use Jinja2 auto-escaping; explicitly sanitize all dynamic content; implement CSP headers; never use safe filter on untrusted data
Detection Method: CSP violation reports; periodic manual XSS testing

Vulnerability: Debug mode enabled in development; stack traces and config exposed on error
Control / Mitigation: Set debug=False in production; create separate dev/prod configs; implement custom error handlers; never expose .env or API keys in error responses
Detection Method: Error page content review; config audit

Vulnerability: No record of who accessed what data or when alerts were modified
Control / Mitigation: Add audit_log table; log all write operations and configuration changes; implement log rotation
Detection Method: Audit log completeness review

Vulnerability: Agent may disconnect silently; alerts lost during network outage
Control / Mitigation: Configure agent keep-alive monitoring; set notify_time and time-reconnect; alert on agent disconnect via Wazuh rule 503
Detection Method: Wazuh agent status dashboard; disconnected agent alerts

Vulnerability: Script has no supervisor; crash silently loses host-based alerts
Control / Mitigation: Run as systemd service with restart policy; implement failed delivery journal; add health check endpoint; queue alerts during outage
Detection Method: Systemd service status; failed delivery file size monitoring

Vulnerability: Docker containers may consume all disk or memory without limits
Control / Mitigation: Set Docker resource limits; configure index lifecycle management; monitor disk usage; set alerting at 80% capacity
Detection Method: Docker stats monitoring; disk utilization alerts

Threat: Lack of monitoring of system failures
Vulnerability: No centralized monitoring of pipeline components or resource utilization
Control / Mitigation: Implement health check script; validate tshark/Flask/Wazuh/SQLite/API status; run every 5 minutes via cron; push status to dashboard /health endpoint
Detection Method: Health check cron output; dashboard health widget

Vulnerability: API connectivity issues or schema changes break alert-to-case pipeline
Control / Mitigation: Validate TheHive API connectivity before forwarding; implement retry with backoff; queue failed alerts; version-pin TheHive API client
Detection Method: Ingestion success rate metric; queued alert count

Vulnerability: Same Wazuh alert forwarded multiple times creates duplicate cases
Control / Mitigation: Use sourceRef deduplication in TheHive; check for existing case before creation; implement idempotent alert handling
Detection Method: Duplicate case detection query

Vulnerability: Cortex analyzer returns wrong enrichment or triggers wrong responder action
Control / Mitigation: Validate analyzer output before acting; require human approval for responder actions above severity 3; log all Cortex decisions for audit
Detection Method: Cortex action audit log; analyst override rate

Vulnerability: Compromised or low-quality MISP feeds introduce bad IOCs
Control / Mitigation: Vet MISP feeds before subscribing; implement IOC confidence scoring; require minimum sighting count before auto-action; audit IOC quality
Detection Method: IOC false positive rate; feed quality metrics

Vulnerability: AI severity mapping produces wrong TheHive case priority
Control / Mitigation: Cross-validate AI risk level with rule severity; allow analyst override of case priority; track reclassification rate
Detection Method: Case reclassification rate tracking

Vulnerability: Detection model degrades as network baselines shift
Control / Mitigation: Schedule monthly model retraining; track precision/recall metrics; alert when detection rate drops below threshold; maintain model version history
Detection Method: Model performance metrics dashboard; detection rate trending

Vulnerability: Attacker manipulates traffic patterns to train model on malicious baselines
Control / Mitigation: Validate training data provenance; require analyst confirmation before data enters training set; implement anomaly detection on training data
Detection Method: Training data audit; model prediction divergence analysis

Vulnerability: ChromaDB persistence fails silently; vector store becomes inconsistent with SQLite
Control / Mitigation: Regular ChromaDB backups alongside SQLite; implement consistency check; add error handling on all vector store operations
Detection Method: Backup verification; consistency check script

Vulnerability: SOC team trusts AI output without verification; critical context missed by all agents
Control / Mitigation: Mandate human review for Critical-severity alerts; display AI confidence prominently; implement AI Disagree workflow; track analyst override rate
Detection Method: Analyst override rate; AI-vs-human decision concordance tracking

Vulnerability: Legitimate IP blocked due to false positive; service disruption
Control / Mitigation: Default to DRY_RUN=true; maintain IP whitelist; require dual-condition trigger for auto-block; implement auto-unblock after review period; log every action with undo command
Detection Method: Blocked IP audit; false block rate tracking; response action log review

Vulnerability: Attacker triggers automated response against arbitrary targets
Control / Mitigation: Rate-limit automated actions (max 5 blocks/hour); require human approval for actions affecting multiple IPs; validate source of trigger; implement cooldown period
Detection Method: Action rate monitoring; manual approval queue depth

Vulnerability: Response scripts run with sudo/root; compromised script = full system access
Control / Mitigation: Run response scripts with minimal privileges; use capabilities instead of full root; validate commands against allowlist; audit script integrity on startup
Detection Method: Script integrity hash verification; privilege audit

Vulnerability: Fully automated loop with no circuit breaker; cascading wrong actions
Control / Mitigation: Implement mandatory human-in-the-loop for Critical severity; add global kill switch on dashboard; cap automated actions per hour; send notifications for every action
Detection Method: Kill switch functionality test; notification delivery verification

Vulnerability: High volume of low-value alerts overwhelms operators; critical alerts buried
Control / Mitigation: Implement alert aggregation; add severity-based notification routing; provide alert summary digest; tune detection thresholds; create Top 10 Critical dashboard view
Detection Method: Alert volume trending; mean-time-to-acknowledge metric; analyst workload dashboard

Threat: Malicious actor exploiting VM internet exposure
Vulnerability: Manual configuration leading to VM escape vulnerabilities
Control / Mitigation: Cost/benefit analysis favours acceptance for solo lab; VMs on host-only networking; VirtualBox updated to latest stable release
Detection Method: VirtualBox version audit; VM network adapter config review

Threat: Accidental leak (GitHub) or local malicious actor
Vulnerability: Hardcoded secrets in configuration files
Control / Mitigation: Secrets management via .env isolation and .gitignore; pre-commit hook scanning for leaked tokens; rotate keys quarterly; never log API keys in plaintext
Detection Method: .gitignore coverage audit; git log secret scan (trufflehog / gitleaks)

Vulnerability: Weak file permissions / missing least privilege
Control / Mitigation: Scripted access control and file ownership restriction (chmod 640); DB file owned by dedicated soc-lab user; no world-readable permissions
Detection Method: File permission audit script; ls -la check on DB path

Threat: Unauthorized device on the local network
Vulnerability: Missing network restrictions / no authentication
Control / Mitigation: Network security management — IP whitelisting via iptables; bind to 127.0.0.1 for local-only or whitelist 192.168.56.0/24; port firewall rules
Detection Method: Port scan of Kali from external interface; firewall rule audit

Threat: Compromised internal host causing alert flood
Vulnerability: Missing outbound API rate limiting / payload filtering
Control / Mitigation: Secure system architecture — rate limits in soc_monitor_v2.py (max 10 API calls/min); payload size cap (4 KB); input validation; alert on anomalous call volume
Detection Method: API call rate dashboard metric; payload size logging

Vulnerability: Missing log rotation / no data retention limits
Control / Mitigation: Capacity management — logrotate config for logs dir; cron job to purge files older than 30 days; disk usage alert at 80%
Detection Method: df -h monitoring; cron job execution log

Threat: Malformed log data causing unhandled exception
Vulnerability: Lack of code exception handling / no service monitoring
Control / Mitigation: Secure system engineering — try/except blocks with structured error logging; run as systemd service with Restart=on-failure; add health check endpoint
Detection Method: systemd service status; error log monitoring; restart count tracking

Threat: Unauthorized user on the local Wi-Fi
Vulnerability: Default administrator credentials left in deployment config
Control / Mitigation: Secure authentication — change default admin/SecretPassword immediately post-deploy; store credentials in .env; enforce password complexity; rotate quarterly
Detection Method: Credential audit; login attempt monitoring via Wazuh Dashboard logs

Vulnerability: Improper threshold tuning causing false positive floods
Control / Mitigation: Change management — test rules in isolation before live deployment; peer review for rule changes; maintain rule changelog; implement rule validation script
Detection Method: Rule change diff review; false positive rate per rule post-deployment

Vulnerability: Missing least privilege / no service tamper protection
Control / Mitigation: Privileged access management — run agent as dedicated ossec user; restrict write access to agent config; enable agent self-monitoring; alert on binary modification
Detection Method: Agent FIM on binaries; service status alerts

Vulnerability: ARP protocol lacks authentication (stateless)
Control / Mitigation: Network security management — static ARP entries for critical hosts; enable ARP spoof detection via RULE-005; monitor for duplicate MACs
Detection Method: ARP table monitoring; Wazuh ARP spoof alerts

Threat: Vendor outage or adverse policy change
Vulnerability: Single point of failure / vendor lock-in
Control / Mitigation: Supplier relationship management — review Airia/OpenAI ToS quarterly; abstract AI calls behind interface layer; document fallback to local LLM (Ollama); monitor vendor status
Detection Method: Vendor status page monitoring; API error rate trending

Vulnerability: Blindly pulling unpinned/unverified package versions
Control / Mitigation: Technical vulnerability management — pin all versions in requirements.txt; run pip-audit before each phase; use hash verification; review dependency changelogs; subscribe to security advisories
Detection Method: pip-audit CI check; dependency version drift monitoring

Threat: Unauthorized access to internal SOC playbooks
Vulnerability: Lack of encryption or access control for documentation files
Control / Mitigation: Information classification — store sensitive playbooks in private repo or encrypted vault; classify documents; never commit credentials to public docs
Detection Method: Repository visibility audit; document classification review

Threat: Accidental upload of PII to AI model
Vulnerability: Lack of automated data masking/anonymization
Control / Mitigation: Data masking and privacy — regex-based PII scrubber before AI submission; log data sent to API; review Airia data retention policy; add PII detection unit test
Detection Method: PII detection test suite; API payload audit sampling
